JMIR Public Health: Acceptability and Feasibility of Longitudinal Sampling for Sexually Transmitted Enteric Infections in #Gay, #Bisexual, and Other Men Who Have Sex With Men (GB#MSM): Prospective Cohort Pilot Study Conducted in 2022 in South East England

external.description: Background: In the last 2 decades, there has been an increasing number of sexually transmissible enteric infection (STEI) outbreaks among #Gay, #Bisexual, or other men who have sex with men (GB#MSM). There remain important gaps in our understanding of how STEI transmission is sustained that repeated collection of samples could help to address. Objective: This study aimed to assess the feasibility and acceptability of longitudinal samples and #Epidemiological data collection among GB#MSM accessing sexual health services (SHS) through a prospective cohort study. Methods: GB#MSM (≥16 years) accessing 2 SHS in Brighton and Sussex were recruited between May and October 2022. Participants provided an initial rectal swab and optional fecal sample and completed an online baseline questionnaire. Weekly follow-up questionnaires and rectal swabs were collected for a further 11 weeks. Sexually transmitted infection (STI) #Surveillance data were pseudonymously linked to provide additional clinical and demographic information. Selected participants were invited to take part in an optional one-to-one interview. We assessed the number completing study procedures, characteristics of those completing procedures and not, and representativeness of the study sample alongside facilitators and barriers from interviews. A chi-square test was used to compare groups. Results: Overall, 193 participants were recruited. Half (100/193, 51.8%) provided a baseline rectal swab, with a third (34/100, 34.0%) of them providing all 12 swabs. Alongside the baseline swab, 76.0% (76/100) provided the optional fecal sample. Just over a third (71/193, 36.8%) of participants completed a baseline questionnaire, with a fifth (15/71, 21.1%) providing all follow-up questionnaires. Interviews (n=21) found that participation was motivated by the feeling of giving something back for services received and a perceived indirect benefit to self. The study was generally accepted, with over half reflecting on the perceived ease of participation and relatively simple tasks that could be easily integrated into normal routine with an element of flexibility. Most participants were satisfied with the 12-week study length, and having a definitive end point aided the ability to assess if they would be able to participate. Barriers to completing the study procedures included not being aware of what was required, particularly for the follow-up questionnaires. Suggested improvements included concise and easier-to-read instructions, with a section to clearly list out the key procedures of the study. SMS text messaging reminders were sent, but these were seen with variable utility and interpreted in different ways (eg, personal or generic reminders or thank you messages). Conclusions: This study has provided evidence that longitudinal rectal swab sampling and data collection for #Research purposes are feasible and acceptable among GB#MSM attending SHS. This provides an innovative way to address important knowledge gaps about STEI transmission, which will help inform #PublicHealth measures for infection control. Tangible insights from this pilot can also inform the design of similar studies in other settings.
external.title: Acceptability and Feasibility of Longitudinal Sampling for Sexually Transmitted Enteric Infections in #Gay, #Bisexual, and Other Men Who Have Sex With Men (GB#MSM): Prospective Cohort Pilot Study Conducted in 2022 in South East England

Title: Petal Tsunami
Style: Modern 3D surrealism echoing the fluid dynamics of Hokusai's "The Great Wave."
Prompt in ALT

images.alt: A modern 3D surreal artwork depicting a massive ocean wave composed entirely of millions of delicate pink cherry blossom petals, frozen mid-crash in dynamic motion over a serene Japanese koi pond. The ethereal petal wave sweeps diagonally from the upper left to lower right, each individual blossom glowing translucent pink under soft, diffused spring sunlight that illuminates them from behind. Beneath the crystal-clear water surface, large ghostly white koi fish with flowing fins swim gracefully upward toward the cascading petals, their forms rendered in darker, richer underwater tones that contrast beautifully with the bright pastel lighting above. The composition merges kinetic energy with delicate tranquility, echoing Hokusai's "The Great Wave" through fluid dynamics while creating a dreamlike boundary where water and flora become one.

images.alt: An ancient Egyptian fresco-style painting of a mythological goddess in a strictly frontal, hieratic pose, her eyes dramatically outlined with thick black kohl and wearing a traditional striped nemes headdress. The left half of her divine form radiates with molten gold and amber tones representing the scorching Sahara sun, while the right half gleams in deep lapis lazuli blue and crystalline silver, embodying sacred supernatural ice. She holds an ornate ankh that burns with golden flames at its top loop and freezes into crystalline ice formations at its base, symbolizing the duality of her nature. Stylized papyrus columns flanking her left side are rendered in warm golds and oranges, while lotus columns on her right are depicted in cool blues and whites, all set against a flat background with harsh, unilateral lighting that creates no soft gradients—golden directional light rigidly illuminating the fire side and cool celestial light defining the ice side.
